---
title: "Salary & Cost of Living"
subtitle: "Evaluate your stipend against local cost of living and advocate for fair compensation"
description: "A worksheet-based guide to assessing PhD stipend purchasing power, city-by-city cost of living adjustments, negotiation strategies, and tax treatment."
order: 1
---

PhD stipends vary widely — from below $18,000 to over $45,000 per year — while the cost of living in university cities ranges just as dramatically. A stipend that sounds reasonable in one city may leave students unable to afford rent, food, or transportation in another. This guide helps you evaluate your real purchasing power and advocate for fair compensation.

---

## 1. Key Questions to Ask Before Accepting an Offer

- [ ] What is the **annual stipend** (pre-tax)?
- [ ] Is the stipend a **fellowship**, a **TA/RA appointment**, or a combination? (Each has different tax treatment.)
- [ ] Does the stipend include a **tuition waiver and fee coverage**? Are mandatory student fees covered?
- [ ] How often is the stipend **reviewed or increased** (e.g., annually, tied to NIH NRSA rates)?
- [ ] Is the stipend **12-month** or does it require summer funding separately?
- [ ] What are the **cost-of-living adjustments** built into multi-year offers?

---

## 2. Cost-of-Living Assessment Worksheet

Use the table below to compare your stipend against your estimated monthly expenses. Fill in the **Estimated** column when evaluating an offer; fill in the **Actual** column after your first month.

| Expense Category | Estimated ($/mo) | Actual ($/mo) | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| Rent (1BR or shared room) | | | Check [Zillow](https://www.zillow.com/), [Apartments.com](https://www.apartments.com/), or local FB housing groups |
| Utilities (electric, gas, water, internet) | | | Typically $100–$200/mo |
| Groceries | | | USDA moderate-cost plan: ~$350–$450/mo |
| Transportation (bus pass / car / bike) | | | Check if university offers discounted transit passes |
| Health insurance premium (if not fully covered) | | | See [Health Insurance guide](health_insurance.qmd) |
| Dental / vision (if not covered) | | | |
| Phone | | | |
| Childcare (if applicable) | | | See [Family Planning guide](family_planning.qmd) |
| Loan repayment (undergraduate debt) | | | |
| Personal care & clothing | | | |
| Entertainment & dining | | | |
| Emergency savings (target 10% of income) | | | |
| **Total Monthly Expenses** | | | |
| **Monthly Stipend (post-tax estimate)** | | | Gross ÷ 12, minus estimated income tax (no SE tax for fellowships — pay quarterly estimated taxes via Form 1040-ES) |
| **Monthly Surplus / (Deficit)** | | | |

### Federal & Benchmark References

| Reference | 2024 Amount |
|---|---|
| NIH NRSA Predoctoral Stipend (F31) | $28,224 / year |
| MIT Living Wage (single adult, Cambridge MA) | ~$46,000 / year |
| Federal Poverty Line (individual) | ~$15,060 / year |
| NSF Graduate Research Fellowship | $37,000 / year |
| Median PhD stipend (US, STEM) | ~$30,000 / year |

> **Rule of thumb:** Your stipend should cover at least the [MIT Living Wage](https://livingwage.mit.edu/) for your city. Use the MIT calculator to find the figure for your specific location.

---

## 3. Adjusting for City Cost of Living

To compare stipends across cities, use the **Cost of Living Index (COLI)**. The table below gives approximate COLI values (US average = 100).

| City | Approx. COLI | Implication for a $30,000 stipend |
|---|---|---|
| Rural Midwest | 85 | Equivalent to ~$35,300 purchasing power |
| Austin, TX | 100 | Face value |
| Seattle, WA | 115 | Equivalent to ~$26,100 purchasing power |
| Boston, MA | 125 | Equivalent to ~$24,000 purchasing power |
| New York City, NY | 145 | Equivalent to ~$20,700 purchasing power |
| San Francisco, CA | 155 | Equivalent to ~$19,400 purchasing power |

**Formula:**  
`Adjusted Purchasing Power = Stipend × (100 / Local COLI)`

**Tools:**

- [NerdWallet Cost of Living Calculator](https://www.nerdwallet.com/cost-of-living-calculator)
- [MIT Living Wage Calculator](https://livingwage.mit.edu/)
- [BLS Consumer Expenditure Survey](https://www.bls.gov/cex/)

---

## 🔢 Interactive Stipend Calculator {#sec-stipend-calculator}

> **💾 Cross-page linked.** Stipend, city, and PhD duration set here are automatically shared with the [Wealth Building](wealth_building.qmd), [Family Planning](family_planning.qmd), and [Career ROI](career_roi.qmd) calculators on other pages.

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
cities_data = [
  {name: "Rural Midwest",      coli: 85,  living_wage: 30000},
  {name: "Raleigh, NC",        coli: 94,  living_wage: 37500},
  {name: "Columbus, OH",       coli: 95,  living_wage: 37000},
  {name: "Austin, TX",         coli: 100, living_wage: 40000},
  {name: "Chicago, IL",        coli: 107, living_wage: 43000},
  {name: "Denver, CO",         coli: 108, living_wage: 44000},
  {name: "Atlanta, GA",        coli: 103, living_wage: 41500},
  {name: "Seattle, WA",        coli: 115, living_wage: 52000},
  {name: "Washington, DC",     coli: 120, living_wage: 55000},
  {name: "Los Angeles, CA",    coli: 122, living_wage: 56000},
  {name: "Boston, MA",         coli: 125, living_wage: 58000},
  {name: "New York City, NY",  coli: 145, living_wage: 68000},
  {name: "San Francisco, CA",  coli: 155, living_wage: 72000}
]
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof annual_stipend = {
  const saved = parseFloat(localStorage.getItem('phd_annual_stipend') || '30000');
  const el = Inputs.range([15000, 60000], {
    label: "Annual Stipend (pre-tax, $)",
    step: 500,
    value: isFinite(saved) ? saved : 30000
  });
  el.addEventListener('input', () => localStorage.setItem('phd_annual_stipend', String(el.value)));
  return el;
}
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof selected_city = {
  const savedName = localStorage.getItem('phd_city') || 'Austin, TX';
  const match = cities_data.find(c => c.name === savedName) || cities_data[3];
  const el = Inputs.select(cities_data, {
    label: "University City",
    format: c => c.name,
    value: match
  });
  const persist = () => {
    localStorage.setItem('phd_city', el.value.name);
    localStorage.setItem('phd_city_coli', String(el.value.coli));
    localStorage.setItem('phd_city_living_wage', String(el.value.living_wage));
  };
  el.addEventListener('change', persist);
  persist();
  return el;
}
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof phd_duration_sal = {
  const saved = parseFloat(localStorage.getItem('phd_duration') || '6');
  const el = Inputs.range([3, 9], {
    label: "Expected PhD Duration (years)",
    step: 0.5,
    value: isFinite(saved) ? saved : 6
  });
  el.addEventListener('input', () => localStorage.setItem('phd_duration', String(el.value)));
  return el;
}
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
monthly_gross = annual_stipend / 12
monthly_net   = Math.round(monthly_gross * 0.88)
adjusted_pp   = Math.round(annual_stipend * (100 / selected_city.coli))

{
  localStorage.setItem('phd_annual_stipend', String(annual_stipend));
  localStorage.setItem('phd_adjusted_pp',    String(adjusted_pp));
}

html`<div style="display:grid;grid-template-columns:repeat(auto-fit,minmax(140px,1fr));gap:10px;margin-top:14px;">
  <div style="padding:14px 10px;border:1px solid var(--bs-border-color,#dee2e6);border-radius:8px;text-align:center;">
    <div style="font-size:.72rem;text-transform:uppercase;letter-spacing:.06em;opacity:.6">Monthly Gross</div>
    <div style="font-size:1.5rem;font-weight:700;margin-top:4px;">$${monthly_gross.toFixed(0)}</div>
  </div>
  <div style="padding:14px 10px;border:1px solid var(--bs-border-color,#dee2e6);border-radius:8px;text-align:center;">
    <div style="font-size:.72rem;text-transform:uppercase;letter-spacing:.06em;opacity:.6">Est. Take-Home/mo</div>
    <div style="font-size:1.5rem;font-weight:700;margin-top:4px;">$${monthly_net}</div>
  </div>
  <div style="padding:14px 10px;border:1px solid var(--bs-border-color,#dee2e6);border-radius:8px;text-align:center;">
    <div style="font-size:.72rem;text-transform:uppercase;letter-spacing:.06em;opacity:.6">Adj. Purchasing Power</div>
    <div style="font-size:1.5rem;font-weight:700;margin-top:4px;">$${adjusted_pp.toLocaleString()}/yr</div>
  </div>
  <div style="padding:14px 10px;border:1px solid var(--bs-border-color,#dee2e6);border-radius:8px;text-align:center;">
    <div style="font-size:.72rem;text-transform:uppercase;letter-spacing:.06em;opacity:.6">COLI Index</div>
    <div style="font-size:1.5rem;font-weight:700;margin-top:4px;">${selected_city.coli}</div>
  </div>
</div>`
```

::: {.panel-tabset}

### 📊 Benchmark Comparison

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
benchmark_data = [
  {label: "Federal Poverty Line",           value: 15060,                       cat: "Reference"},
  {label: "NIH NRSA Predoc (F31, 2024)",    value: 28224,                       cat: "Federal Benchmark"},
  {label: "Median PhD Stipend (STEM)",       value: 30000,                       cat: "Federal Benchmark"},
  {label: "NSF Graduate Fellowship (2024)",  value: 37000,                       cat: "Federal Benchmark"},
  {label: "MIT Living Wage — " + selected_city.name, value: selected_city.living_wage, cat: "Living Wage"},
  {label: "Your Annual Stipend",             value: annual_stipend,              cat: "You"}
]

Plot.plot({
  title: "Your Stipend vs. Key Benchmarks",
  width: 640,
  height: 310,
  marginLeft: 245,
  x: {label: "Annual $ →", tickFormat: d => `$${(d/1000).toFixed(0)}k`},
  color: {
    domain: ["Reference", "Federal Benchmark", "Living Wage", "You"],
    range: ["#e74c3c", "#3498db", "#27ae60", "#f39c12"],
    legend: true
  },
  marks: [
    Plot.barX(benchmark_data, {
      x: "value", y: "label", fill: "cat",
      sort: {y: "x"}, rx: 3, tip: true
    }),
    Plot.ruleX([0])
  ]
})
```

### 🌎 City Purchasing Power

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
// Compare nominal stipend vs. nominal living wage for each city (same dollar basis)
city_comparison_data = cities_data.map(c => ({
  city: c.name,
  nominal_stipend: annual_stipend,
  living_wage: c.living_wage,
  // Purchasing power shown separately in stat cards; sufficiency = can your stipend cover local living wage?
  sufficient: annual_stipend >= c.living_wage
}))

Plot.plot({
  title: `Does $${annual_stipend.toLocaleString()}/yr Meet the Living Wage in Each City?`,
  subtitle: "Tick mark (|) shows each city's MIT living wage. Green bar = stipend meets or exceeds it.",
  width: 660,
  height: 380,
  marginLeft: 165,
  x: {label: "Annual $ →", tickFormat: d => `$${(d/1000).toFixed(0)}k`},
  color: {
    domain: [true, false],
    range: ["#27ae60", "#e74c3c"],
    legend: true,
    tickFormat: d => d ? "≥ Living Wage ✓" : "< Living Wage ✗"
  },
  marks: [
    Plot.barX(city_comparison_data, {
      x: "nominal_stipend", y: "city", fill: "sufficient",
      sort: {y: "-living_wage"}, rx: 3, tip: true
    }),
    Plot.tickX(city_comparison_data, {
      x: "living_wage", y: "city",
      stroke: "#1a1a1a", strokeWidth: 2.5
    }),
    Plot.ruleX([0])
  ]
})
```

### 📆 PhD Stipend vs. Living Wage Over Time

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
phd_years_range = Array.from({length: Math.ceil(phd_duration_sal) + 1}, (_, i) => i)

stipend_timeline = phd_years_range.flatMap(y => [
  // 2% annual raise = approximate average academic stipend COLA (below CPI, but common in practice)
  {year: y, value: Math.round(annual_stipend * Math.pow(1.02, y)),       series: "Your Stipend (2% annual raise)"},
  {year: y, value: selected_city.living_wage,                             series: "MIT Living Wage (" + selected_city.name + ")"},
  // NIH NRSA 2024 base = $28,224; also projected at 2% to show policy trajectory
  {year: y, value: Math.round(28224 * Math.pow(1.02, y)),                series: "NIH NRSA Benchmark"}
])

Plot.plot({
  title: "Stipend Trajectory Over Your PhD Duration",
  width: 600,
  height: 300,
  x: {label: "Year of PhD", tickFormat: d => `Yr ${d}`},
  y: {label: "Annual Amount ($) →", tickFormat: d => `$${(d/1000).toFixed(0)}k`},
  color: {
    domain: ["Your Stipend (2% annual raise)", "MIT Living Wage (" + selected_city.name + ")", "NIH NRSA Benchmark"],
    range: ["#f39c12", "#27ae60", "#3498db"],
    legend: true
  },
  marks: [
    Plot.line(stipend_timeline, {
      x: "year", y: "value", stroke: "series",
      strokeWidth: d => d.series.startsWith("Your") ? 3 : 1.8,
      strokeDash: d => d.series.startsWith("MIT") ? [5, 3] : [],
      tip: true
    }),
    Plot.dot(stipend_timeline.filter(d => d.series.startsWith("Your")), {
      x: "year", y: "value", fill: "#f39c12", r: 4
    })
  ]
})
```

:::

---

## 4. Negotiating Your Stipend

Most PhD students don't realize stipends are negotiable — especially at offer time. Here are evidence-based strategies:

### Before negotiating
- [ ] Research peer institutions' stipend levels (most are publicly posted).
- [ ] Look up your city's MIT Living Wage and bring it to the conversation.
- [ ] Check whether your program has recently received new training grant funding (T32, NRSA), which can increase what PIs and programs can offer.
- [ ] Ask your potential advisor about supplemental RA funding, travel awards, and conference support.

### At the negotiation
- [ ] Frame the ask around cost of living, not personal need: *"I've researched that the living wage in [city] is $X, and I want to make sure I can focus fully on research."*
- [ ] Ask about guaranteed annual increases: *"Is there a policy for annual stipend adjustments, and if so, what is the typical percentage?"*
- [ ] If the stipend can't be raised, ask for alternatives: summer support, fee waivers, childcare subsidies, or professional development funds.

### After accepting
- [ ] Set a calendar reminder to revisit your stipend each year relative to inflation (CPI) and local cost-of-living changes.
- [ ] Advocate collectively through your graduate student union or student government for program-wide increases.

---

## 5. Taxes on Graduate Stipends

Stipend taxation depends on how you are funded:

| Funding Type | Federal Tax | FICA (Social Security + Medicare) |
|---|---|---|
| Fellowship / scholarship (F31, NSF GRF) | Yes — you must pay estimated quarterly taxes | Generally exempt |
| TA / RA appointment (W-2) | Yes — withheld automatically | Generally exempt if enrolled half-time |
| Industry-funded RA | Depends on contract terms | May apply |

> **Action:** File Form 1040-ES quarterly to avoid underpayment penalties if you receive a fellowship. See IRS Publication 970.

**Resources:**

- [IRS Publication 970: Tax Benefits for Education](https://www.irs.gov/pub/irs-pdf/p970.pdf)
- [GradTaxes.com](https://www.gradtaxes.com/) — tax guides specifically for graduate students
- Your university's international student office (if applicable) for treaty exemptions

---

## 6. Advocacy & External Resources

- [National Science Policy Network (NSPN)](https://scipolnetwork.org/)
- [UAW Graduate Student Union campaigns](https://uaw.org/)
- [Future of Research — stipend tracking data](https://futureofresearch.org/)
- [Council of Graduate Schools](https://cgsnet.org/)

---

## 🗺️ University Cost-of-Living Map {#sec-university-map}

Compare stipend sufficiency across **85 major US research universities** — for an **individual** and for a **family of four with a dual PhD income** (both partners are PhD students at the same institution, two children under 10). A ratio ≥ 1.0 means the stipend(s) fully cover the local living wage.

> **Family scenario:** Both partners earn a PhD stipend → household income = 2 × stipend. The threshold is the [MIT Living Wage](https://livingwage.mit.edu/) for 2 working adults + 2 children in that metro area. Childcare costs are the largest additional expense. **Stipend and living-wage values are approximate 2024 estimates.**

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
topojson_lib = require("topojson-client@3")
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
us_atlas   = fetch("https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/us-atlas@3/states-10m.json").then(r => r.json())
us_states_geo = topojson_lib.feature(us_atlas, us_atlas.objects.states)
us_nation_geo = topojson_lib.feature(us_atlas, us_atlas.objects.nation)
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
// 85 major US research universities
// stipend : typical STEM PhD annual stipend (pre-tax, 2024 approx.)
// lw1     : annual living wage — 1 adult, 0 children (MIT Living Wage, local)
// lw4     : annual HOUSEHOLD living wage — 2 working adults + 2 children (MIT LW, total)
univ_map_data = [
  // Northeast — Very High Cost
  {name:"MIT",               city:"Cambridge",       state:"MA", lat:42.36, lon:-71.09, stipend:42840, lw1:44000, lw4:118000},
  {name:"Harvard",           city:"Cambridge",       state:"MA", lat:42.37, lon:-71.12, stipend:42840, lw1:44000, lw4:118000},
  {name:"Boston University", city:"Boston",          state:"MA", lat:42.35, lon:-71.10, stipend:36000, lw1:44000, lw4:118000},
  {name:"Tufts",             city:"Medford",         state:"MA", lat:42.41, lon:-71.12, stipend:38000, lw1:44000, lw4:118000},
  {name:"Northeastern",      city:"Boston",          state:"MA", lat:42.34, lon:-71.09, stipend:34000, lw1:44000, lw4:118000},
  {name:"Columbia",          city:"New York",        state:"NY", lat:40.81, lon:-73.96, stipend:40000, lw1:58000, lw4:148000},
  {name:"NYU",               city:"New York",        state:"NY", lat:40.73, lon:-74.00, stipend:38000, lw1:58000, lw4:148000},
  {name:"Cornell",           city:"Ithaca",          state:"NY", lat:42.45, lon:-76.48, stipend:34000, lw1:32000, lw4:86000},
  {name:"SUNY Stony Brook",  city:"Stony Brook",     state:"NY", lat:40.91, lon:-73.12, stipend:35000, lw1:45000, lw4:120000},
  {name:"Rochester",         city:"Rochester",       state:"NY", lat:43.13, lon:-77.63, stipend:28000, lw1:31000, lw4:83000},
  {name:"Syracuse",          city:"Syracuse",        state:"NY", lat:43.04, lon:-76.13, stipend:26000, lw1:31000, lw4:82000},
  {name:"Princeton",         city:"Princeton",       state:"NJ", lat:40.35, lon:-74.66, stipend:42000, lw1:43000, lw4:114000},
  {name:"Rutgers",           city:"New Brunswick",   state:"NJ", lat:40.50, lon:-74.45, stipend:30000, lw1:42000, lw4:110000},
  {name:"Yale",              city:"New Haven",       state:"CT", lat:41.31, lon:-72.92, stipend:38000, lw1:40000, lw4:106000},
  {name:"Brown",             city:"Providence",      state:"RI", lat:41.83, lon:-71.40, stipend:35000, lw1:38000, lw4:100000},
  {name:"Dartmouth",         city:"Hanover",         state:"NH", lat:43.70, lon:-72.29, stipend:35000, lw1:33000, lw4:87000},
  // Mid-Atlantic
  {name:"Penn",              city:"Philadelphia",    state:"PA", lat:39.95, lon:-75.19, stipend:38000, lw1:36000, lw4:96000},
  {name:"Temple",            city:"Philadelphia",    state:"PA", lat:39.98, lon:-75.15, stipend:26000, lw1:36000, lw4:96000},
  {name:"Drexel",            city:"Philadelphia",    state:"PA", lat:39.96, lon:-75.19, stipend:28000, lw1:36000, lw4:96000},
  {name:"Carnegie Mellon",   city:"Pittsburgh",      state:"PA", lat:40.44, lon:-79.99, stipend:38000, lw1:33000, lw4:87000},
  {name:"Pittsburgh",        city:"Pittsburgh",      state:"PA", lat:40.44, lon:-79.96, stipend:28000, lw1:33000, lw4:87000},
  {name:"Penn State",        city:"State College",   state:"PA", lat:40.80, lon:-77.86, stipend:24000, lw1:29000, lw4:77000},
  {name:"Johns Hopkins",     city:"Baltimore",       state:"MD", lat:39.33, lon:-76.62, stipend:36000, lw1:36000, lw4:96000},
  {name:"Georgetown",        city:"Washington",      state:"DC", lat:38.91, lon:-77.07, stipend:38000, lw1:46000, lw4:122000},
  {name:"George Washington", city:"Washington",      state:"DC", lat:38.90, lon:-77.05, stipend:36000, lw1:46000, lw4:122000},
  {name:"Virginia",          city:"Charlottesville", state:"VA", lat:38.03, lon:-78.51, stipend:28000, lw1:33000, lw4:87000},
  {name:"Virginia Tech",     city:"Blacksburg",      state:"VA", lat:37.23, lon:-80.42, stipend:24000, lw1:29000, lw4:77000},
  {name:"West Virginia",     city:"Morgantown",      state:"WV", lat:39.63, lon:-79.96, stipend:22000, lw1:27000, lw4:72000},
  {name:"Case Western",      city:"Cleveland",       state:"OH", lat:41.50, lon:-81.61, stipend:28000, lw1:30000, lw4:80000},
  {name:"Ohio State",        city:"Columbus",        state:"OH", lat:40.00, lon:-83.02, stipend:24000, lw1:29000, lw4:78000},
  // Southeast
  {name:"Duke",              city:"Durham",          state:"NC", lat:35.99, lon:-78.93, stipend:35000, lw1:32000, lw4:84000},
  {name:"UNC Chapel Hill",   city:"Chapel Hill",     state:"NC", lat:35.90, lon:-79.05, stipend:28000, lw1:32000, lw4:84000},
  {name:"NC State",          city:"Raleigh",         state:"NC", lat:35.78, lon:-78.68, stipend:26000, lw1:32000, lw4:84000},
  {name:"Vanderbilt",        city:"Nashville",       state:"TN", lat:36.14, lon:-86.80, stipend:33000, lw1:31000, lw4:82000},
  {name:"Tennessee",         city:"Knoxville",       state:"TN", lat:35.96, lon:-83.92, stipend:22000, lw1:29000, lw4:77000},
  {name:"Georgia Tech",      city:"Atlanta",         state:"GA", lat:33.77, lon:-84.40, stipend:34000, lw1:35000, lw4:92000},
  {name:"Emory",             city:"Atlanta",         state:"GA", lat:33.79, lon:-84.32, stipend:34000, lw1:35000, lw4:92000},
  {name:"Georgia",           city:"Athens",          state:"GA", lat:33.95, lon:-83.37, stipend:26000, lw1:30000, lw4:80000},
  {name:"Florida",           city:"Gainesville",     state:"FL", lat:29.65, lon:-82.35, stipend:25000, lw1:31000, lw4:82000},
  {name:"FSU",               city:"Tallahassee",     state:"FL", lat:30.44, lon:-84.30, stipend:24000, lw1:30000, lw4:80000},
  {name:"Miami",             city:"Coral Gables",    state:"FL", lat:25.72, lon:-80.28, stipend:30000, lw1:36000, lw4:96000},
  {name:"UCF",               city:"Orlando",         state:"FL", lat:28.60, lon:-81.20, stipend:22000, lw1:32000, lw4:85000},
  {name:"USF",               city:"Tampa",           state:"FL", lat:28.06, lon:-82.41, stipend:22000, lw1:32000, lw4:85000},
  {name:"South Carolina",    city:"Columbia",        state:"SC", lat:33.99, lon:-81.03, stipend:22000, lw1:29000, lw4:77000},
  {name:"Clemson",           city:"Clemson",         state:"SC", lat:34.68, lon:-82.84, stipend:22000, lw1:28000, lw4:74000},
  {name:"Alabama",           city:"Tuscaloosa",      state:"AL", lat:33.21, lon:-87.54, stipend:22000, lw1:27000, lw4:71000},
  {name:"Auburn",            city:"Auburn",          state:"AL", lat:32.60, lon:-85.49, stipend:22000, lw1:27000, lw4:71000},
  {name:"LSU",               city:"Baton Rouge",     state:"LA", lat:30.41, lon:-91.18, stipend:22000, lw1:29000, lw4:77000},
  {name:"Tulane",            city:"New Orleans",     state:"LA", lat:29.94, lon:-90.12, stipend:30000, lw1:32000, lw4:85000},
  {name:"Mississippi",       city:"Oxford",          state:"MS", lat:34.37, lon:-89.53, stipend:20000, lw1:26000, lw4:69000},
  // Midwest
  {name:"Chicago",           city:"Chicago",         state:"IL", lat:41.79, lon:-87.60, stipend:40000, lw1:39000, lw4:104000},
  {name:"Northwestern",      city:"Evanston",        state:"IL", lat:42.05, lon:-87.68, stipend:38000, lw1:39000, lw4:104000},
  {name:"Illinois",          city:"Urbana-Champaign",state:"IL", lat:40.11, lon:-88.21, stipend:26000, lw1:30000, lw4:80000},
  {name:"Notre Dame",        city:"South Bend",      state:"IN", lat:41.70, lon:-86.24, stipend:32000, lw1:29000, lw4:77000},
  {name:"Purdue",            city:"West Lafayette",  state:"IN", lat:40.43, lon:-86.92, stipend:24000, lw1:28000, lw4:74000},
  {name:"Indiana",           city:"Bloomington",     state:"IN", lat:39.17, lon:-86.52, stipend:22000, lw1:28000, lw4:74000},
  {name:"Michigan",          city:"Ann Arbor",       state:"MI", lat:42.28, lon:-83.74, stipend:32000, lw1:33000, lw4:87000},
  {name:"Michigan State",    city:"East Lansing",    state:"MI", lat:42.73, lon:-84.48, stipend:26000, lw1:31000, lw4:82000},
  {name:"Wisconsin",         city:"Madison",         state:"WI", lat:43.07, lon:-89.41, stipend:29000, lw1:33000, lw4:87000},
  {name:"Minnesota",         city:"Minneapolis",     state:"MN", lat:44.97, lon:-93.23, stipend:28000, lw1:35000, lw4:92000},
  {name:"Iowa",              city:"Iowa City",       state:"IA", lat:41.66, lon:-91.53, stipend:22000, lw1:28000, lw4:74000},
  {name:"Iowa State",        city:"Ames",            state:"IA", lat:42.03, lon:-93.63, stipend:22000, lw1:28000, lw4:73000},
  {name:"Nebraska",          city:"Lincoln",         state:"NE", lat:40.82, lon:-96.70, stipend:22000, lw1:27000, lw4:71000},
  {name:"Kansas",            city:"Lawrence",        state:"KS", lat:38.96, lon:-95.25, stipend:22000, lw1:27000, lw4:72000},
  {name:"Missouri",          city:"Columbia",        state:"MO", lat:38.95, lon:-92.33, stipend:22000, lw1:27000, lw4:72000},
  {name:"Wash. U. St. Louis",city:"St. Louis",       state:"MO", lat:38.65, lon:-90.31, stipend:38000, lw1:31000, lw4:83000},
  // South & Plains
  {name:"UT Austin",         city:"Austin",          state:"TX", lat:30.29, lon:-97.74, stipend:33000, lw1:36000, lw4:96000},
  {name:"Texas A&M",         city:"College Station", state:"TX", lat:30.62, lon:-96.34, stipend:27000, lw1:28000, lw4:74000},
  {name:"Rice",              city:"Houston",         state:"TX", lat:29.72, lon:-95.40, stipend:36000, lw1:34000, lw4:90000},
  {name:"Houston",           city:"Houston",         state:"TX", lat:29.73, lon:-95.42, stipend:24000, lw1:34000, lw4:90000},
  {name:"Oklahoma",          city:"Norman",          state:"OK", lat:35.21, lon:-97.44, stipend:22000, lw1:28000, lw4:74000},
  {name:"Arkansas",          city:"Fayetteville",    state:"AR", lat:36.07, lon:-94.17, stipend:22000, lw1:28000, lw4:74000},
  {name:"Kansas State",      city:"Manhattan",       state:"KS", lat:39.18, lon:-96.58, stipend:22000, lw1:27000, lw4:72000},
  // Mountain West
  {name:"Colorado",          city:"Boulder",         state:"CO", lat:40.01, lon:-105.27, stipend:32000, lw1:38000, lw4:100000},
  {name:"Colorado State",    city:"Fort Collins",    state:"CO", lat:40.57, lon:-105.08, stipend:26000, lw1:35000, lw4:93000},
  {name:"Denver",            city:"Denver",          state:"CO", lat:39.68, lon:-104.96, stipend:28000, lw1:38000, lw4:100000},
  {name:"Arizona",           city:"Tucson",          state:"AZ", lat:32.23, lon:-110.95, stipend:26000, lw1:31000, lw4:82000},
  {name:"ASU",               city:"Tempe",           state:"AZ", lat:33.42, lon:-111.94, stipend:26000, lw1:33000, lw4:87000},
  {name:"Utah",              city:"Salt Lake City",  state:"UT", lat:40.76, lon:-111.85, stipend:26000, lw1:33000, lw4:87000},
  {name:"New Mexico",        city:"Albuquerque",     state:"NM", lat:35.08, lon:-106.62, stipend:22000, lw1:29000, lw4:78000},
  {name:"Nevada Reno",       city:"Reno",            state:"NV", lat:39.54, lon:-119.82, stipend:24000, lw1:33000, lw4:87000},
  // West Coast
  {name:"Stanford",          city:"Palo Alto",       state:"CA", lat:37.43, lon:-122.17, stipend:46800, lw1:55000, lw4:145000},
  {name:"Caltech",           city:"Pasadena",        state:"CA", lat:34.14, lon:-118.13, stipend:41000, lw1:50000, lw4:130000},
  {name:"UC Berkeley",       city:"Berkeley",        state:"CA", lat:37.87, lon:-122.27, stipend:42000, lw1:52000, lw4:138000},
  {name:"UCLA",              city:"Los Angeles",     state:"CA", lat:34.07, lon:-118.44, stipend:35000, lw1:48000, lw4:128000},
  {name:"UCSD",              city:"San Diego",       state:"CA", lat:32.88, lon:-117.23, stipend:38000, lw1:46000, lw4:122000},
  {name:"UCSB",              city:"Santa Barbara",   state:"CA", lat:34.41, lon:-119.85, stipend:36000, lw1:47000, lw4:124000},
  {name:"UC Davis",          city:"Davis",           state:"CA", lat:38.54, lon:-121.74, stipend:35000, lw1:42000, lw4:110000},
  {name:"UC Irvine",         city:"Irvine",          state:"CA", lat:33.64, lon:-117.84, stipend:34000, lw1:46000, lw4:122000},
  {name:"UC Riverside",      city:"Riverside",       state:"CA", lat:33.97, lon:-117.33, stipend:32000, lw1:40000, lw4:106000},
  {name:"UCSF",              city:"San Francisco",   state:"CA", lat:37.76, lon:-122.46, stipend:43000, lw1:55000, lw4:142000},
  {name:"USC",               city:"Los Angeles",     state:"CA", lat:34.02, lon:-118.28, stipend:35000, lw1:48000, lw4:128000},
  {name:"Washington",        city:"Seattle",         state:"WA", lat:47.65, lon:-122.31, stipend:37000, lw1:48000, lw4:128000},
  {name:"Oregon",            city:"Eugene",          state:"OR", lat:44.04, lon:-123.07, stipend:26000, lw1:36000, lw4:95000},
  {name:"Oregon State",      city:"Corvallis",       state:"OR", lat:44.56, lon:-123.28, stipend:24000, lw1:33000, lw4:87000},
  {name:"Hawaii",            city:"Honolulu",        state:"HI", lat:21.30, lon:-157.82, stipend:28000, lw1:48000, lw4:126000},
  {name:"Alaska Fairbanks",  city:"Fairbanks",       state:"AK", lat:64.86, lon:-147.83, stipend:22000, lw1:38000, lw4:100000},
]
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof map_scenario = Inputs.radio(
  ["👤 Individual", "👨‍👩‍👧‍👦 Family of 4 (both PhD students, 2 kids)"],
  {label: "Scenario:", value: "👤 Individual"}
)
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
is_fam       = map_scenario.includes("Family")
univ_ratios  = univ_map_data.map(u => {
  const income = is_fam ? 2 * u.stipend : u.stipend
  const lw     = is_fam ? u.lw4 : u.lw1
  const ratio  = income / lw
  return {
    ...u,
    income,
    lw,
    ratio,
    color: ratio >= 1.25 ? "#1a9641"
         : ratio >= 1.0  ? "#78c679"
         : ratio >= 0.8  ? "#d9f0a3"
         : ratio >= 0.6  ? "#fe9929"
         :                  "#cc4c02",
    badge: ratio >= 1.25 ? "✅ Comfortable (≥125%)"
         : ratio >= 1.0  ? "✅ Sufficient (100–125%)"
         : ratio >= 0.8  ? "⚠️ Close (80–99%)"
         : ratio >= 0.6  ? "🔶 Insufficient (60–79%)"
         :                  "🔴 Severe shortfall (<60%)",
    shortfall: income < lw ? Math.round(lw - income) : 0
  }
}).sort((a, b) => b.ratio - a.ratio)
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
// Color legend
html`<div style="display:flex;flex-wrap:wrap;gap:14px;align-items:center;margin:10px 0 4px;">
  <strong style="font-size:.85rem;">Ratio Legend:</strong>
  ${[
    {color:"#1a9641", label:"≥ 1.25× Comfortable"},
    {color:"#78c679", label:"1.0–1.25× Sufficient"},
    {color:"#d9f0a3", label:"0.8–1.0× Close"},
    {color:"#fe9929", label:"0.6–0.8× Insufficient"},
    {color:"#cc4c02", label:"< 0.6× Severe shortfall"}
  ].map(e => html`<span style="display:inline-flex;align-items:center;gap:5px;font-size:.82rem;">
    <span style="width:13px;height:13px;border-radius:50%;background:${e.color};border:1px solid #555;flex-shrink:0;"></span>${e.label}
  </span>`)}
</div>`
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
Plot.plot({
  projection: "albers-usa",
  width: 860,
  height: 500,
  style: {background: "transparent", overflow: "visible"},
  marks: [
    Plot.geo(us_states_geo, {stroke: "#bbb", strokeWidth: 0.6, fill: "#f5f5f5"}),
    Plot.geo(us_nation_geo, {stroke: "#888", strokeWidth: 1.4, fill: "none"}),
    Plot.dot(univ_ratios, {
      x: "lon",
      y: "lat",
      r: 7,
      fill: "color",
      stroke: "white",
      strokeWidth: 1,
      opacity: 0.88,
      title: d => [
        `${d.name}`,
        `${d.city}, ${d.state}`,
        ``,
        `Stipend: $${d.stipend.toLocaleString()}/yr`,
        is_fam ? `Household income (2×): $${d.income.toLocaleString()}/yr` : "",
        `Living wage threshold: $${d.lw.toLocaleString()}/yr`,
        `Ratio: ${d.ratio.toFixed(2)}× — ${d.badge}`,
        d.shortfall > 0 ? `Annual shortfall: $${d.shortfall.toLocaleString()}` : `Surplus: $${(d.income - d.lw).toLocaleString()}`
      ].filter(Boolean).join("\n")
    })
  ]
})
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
// Summary stat cards
n_sufficient   = univ_ratios.filter(u => u.ratio >= 1.0).length
n_close        = univ_ratios.filter(u => u.ratio >= 0.8 && u.ratio < 1.0).length
n_insufficient = univ_ratios.filter(u => u.ratio < 0.8).length
median_ratio   = (() => {
  const n = univ_ratios.length
  return n % 2 === 0
    ? (univ_ratios[n/2 - 1].ratio + univ_ratios[n/2].ratio) / 2
    : univ_ratios[Math.floor(n/2)].ratio
})()
best_uni       = univ_ratios[0]
worst_uni      = univ_ratios[univ_ratios.length - 1]

html`<div style="display:grid;grid-template-columns:repeat(auto-fit,minmax(145px,1fr));gap:10px;margin:14px 0;">
  <div style="padding:12px 10px;border:1px solid var(--bs-border-color,#dee2e6);border-radius:8px;text-align:center;">
    <div style="font-size:.72rem;text-transform:uppercase;letter-spacing:.06em;opacity:.6">${is_fam ? "Both stipends sufficient" : "Stipend sufficient"}</div>
    <div style="font-size:1.6rem;font-weight:700;color:#1a9641;margin-top:4px;">${n_sufficient} <span style="font-size:1rem;">/ 85</span></div>
  </div>
  <div style="padding:12px 10px;border:1px solid var(--bs-border-color,#dee2e6);border-radius:8px;text-align:center;">
    <div style="font-size:.72rem;text-transform:uppercase;letter-spacing:.06em;opacity:.6">Close (80–99%)</div>
    <div style="font-size:1.6rem;font-weight:700;color:#fe9929;margin-top:4px;">${n_close} <span style="font-size:1rem;">/ 85</span></div>
  </div>
  <div style="padding:12px 10px;border:1px solid var(--bs-border-color,#dee2e6);border-radius:8px;text-align:center;">
    <div style="font-size:.72rem;text-transform:uppercase;letter-spacing:.06em;opacity:.6">Insufficient (&lt;80%)</div>
    <div style="font-size:1.6rem;font-weight:700;color:#cc4c02;margin-top:4px;">${n_insufficient} <span style="font-size:1rem;">/ 85</span></div>
  </div>
  <div style="padding:12px 10px;border:1px solid var(--bs-border-color,#dee2e6);border-radius:8px;text-align:center;">
    <div style="font-size:.72rem;text-transform:uppercase;letter-spacing:.06em;opacity:.6">Median ratio</div>
    <div style="font-size:1.6rem;font-weight:700;margin-top:4px;">${median_ratio.toFixed(2)}×</div>
  </div>
  <div style="padding:12px 10px;border:1px solid var(--bs-border-color,#dee2e6);border-radius:8px;text-align:center;">
    <div style="font-size:.72rem;text-transform:uppercase;letter-spacing:.06em;opacity:.6">Best ratio</div>
    <div style="font-size:1.1rem;font-weight:700;color:#1a9641;margin-top:4px;">${best_uni.name} (${best_uni.ratio.toFixed(2)}×)</div>
  </div>
  <div style="padding:12px 10px;border:1px solid var(--bs-border-color,#dee2e6);border-radius:8px;text-align:center;">
    <div style="font-size:.72rem;text-transform:uppercase;letter-spacing:.06em;opacity:.6">Worst ratio</div>
    <div style="font-size:1.1rem;font-weight:700;color:#cc4c02;margin-top:4px;">${worst_uni.name} (${worst_uni.ratio.toFixed(2)}×)</div>
  </div>
</div>`
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
// Sortable ranked table — "Income" = stipend for Individual, 2× stipend for Family scenario
html`<p style="font-size:.85rem;opacity:.7;margin-bottom:4px;">
  <em>Income column = ${is_fam ? "household income (2 × stipend)" : "individual stipend"}. Click any column header to sort.</em>
</p>`
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
Inputs.table(
  univ_ratios.map(u => ({
    University: u.name,
    "City, State": `${u.city}, ${u.state}`,
    "Stipend/yr": `$${u.stipend.toLocaleString()}`,
    "Income": `$${u.income.toLocaleString()}`,
    "Living Wage": `$${u.lw.toLocaleString()}`,
    "Ratio": u.ratio.toFixed(2) + "×",
    "Rating": u.badge
  })),
  {rows: 20, sort: "Ratio", reverse: true}
)
```

---

*Last reviewed: 2024. Update this document annually as federal stipend benchmarks and local cost-of-living figures change.*
